title: Hyderabad Flood Simulation
description: Extreme 350mm rainfall in 12 hours. Musi River overflowing. Begumpet and Nampally residential zones severely flooded. 220+ stranded on rooftops.

alert_actions.field_team: DEPLOY IMMEDIATE: All water rescue and heli-evac units dispatch to Begumpet coordinate zone. Critical medical triage active.
alert_actions.hospital: MCI LEVEL 1: Gandhi and Osmania Hospitals activate mass casualty protocols. Clear ER bays for 220 incoming casualties.
alert_actions.public: RED ALERT: Begumpet and surrounding areas are under severe flood warning. Move to upper floors immediately. Avoid low-lying roads.

risk_warnings: Water level rise of 1.2m expected in next 2 hours
Sub-station failure warning - absolute power blackout in Begumpet

agent_decisions.output: Classified: FLOOD. Severity: 5/5. Major risk zone defined around Hyderabad Central Basin.
Water levels at +2.4m. 8 critical residential zones inundated. Drainage capacity exceeded.
Calculated 92% survival probability. Estimated count: 220 survivors requiring immediate rooftop extraction.
Medical urgency: CRITICAL. Pre-positioning hypothermia blankets, trauma packs, and anti-venoms.
Assigned highest dispatch rank: P1. Score: 0.95. Launch immediate response window of <10 minutes.
Allocated: 12 ambulances, 8 NDRF teams, 2 rescue choppers, 6 motorboats.
Primary route: Gandhi Hospital (140 patients). Secondary route: Osmania Hospital (80 patients).
Secondary hazards identified: Structural collapse of old structures, electrocution risk, and cholera risk.
Drafted emergency cell broadcast, police command SMS, and hospital disaster team pages.
RESCUE PLAN FINALIZED. P1 dispatch approved. Field commanders take control. Deploying active units now.

title: Warangal Industrial Fire
description: Chemical storage tank fire at Hanamkonda Industrial area. Risk of toxic vapor cloud. 45 staff stranded in nearby administrative block.

alert_actions.field_team: FIRE COMBAT DISPATCH: 6 engines deploy to chemical site. NDRF team evacuate admin block wearing Hazmat gear.
alert_actions.hospital: BURN HAZARD ALERT: MGM Hospital Warangal prepare for 45 toxic inhalation and thermal burn patients.
alert_actions.public: SHELTER IN PLACE: Toxic chemical plume heading north. Close all windows and turn off AC units within 1km radius.

risk_warnings: High danger of BLEVE (Boiling Liquid Expanding Vapor Explosion)
Toxic chemical plume (sulfur dioxide) dispersion warning
Wind direction shifting north-east at 14 km/h

agent_decisions.output: Classified: CHEMICAL FIRE. Severity: 4/5. 500m exclusion zone established.
Class 3 flammables involved. Admin building trapped by heat corridor. 45 employees unaccounted for.
Calculated 82% survival chance. Estimated: 45 casualties trapped inside air-conditioned office block.
Triage priority: HIGH. Focus on toxic fume inhalation, smoke asphyxia, and second-degree thermal burns.
Assigned priority: P2. Score: 0.81. Response timeframe: 5 minutes.
Dispatched: 6 foam tenders, 5 ambulances, 3 Hazmat rescue teams, 2 mobile ICU triage units.
Routed all 45 patients to MGM Hospital Warangal (nearest burns and toxicology specialty).
Secondary hazards: Tank 4 structural failure, acid run-off into local waterways, vapor explosion.
Sent localized Hazmat alert notifications to industrial park employees and community siren controls.
RESCUE PLAN CONCLUDED. P2 containment and evacuation underway. Foam blankets being applied.

title: Highway Bridge Collapse
description: Highway flyover deck collapse near Medchal Bypass. Multiple cars trapped underneath. Concrete debris blocking NH 44.

alert_actions.field_team: COLLAPSE RESCUE TEAM DISPATCH: Heavy search and rescue teams deploy to Medchal with concrete cutters and cameras.
alert_actions.hospital: TRAUMA LEVEL 2: Gandhi Hospital prepare for crush syndrome cases. Hitec City hospital standby for overflow.
alert_actions.public: TRAFFIC ALERT: NH 44 blocked at Medchal. Commuters diversion through Outer Ring Road Exit 6. Gridlock warning.

risk_warnings: Unstable adjacent bridge pillar structural collapse risk
Crush syndrome hazard for victims trapped over 2 hours
Severe traffic gridlock delaying second wave rescue units

agent_decisions.output: Classified: STRUCTURAL COLLAPSE. Severity: 4/5. 200m zone isolated.
60-meter concrete segment collapsed. 8 passenger cars confirmed crushed or trapped in the void.
Calculated 78% survivor probability based on void spaces. 30 victims estimated trapped.
Triage Priority: HIGH. High probability of crush injuries, orthopedic trauma, internal bleeding.
Priority: P2. Score: 0.79. Heavy rescue crane transit dispatch priority active.
Allocated: 5 specialized USAR teams with cutters/shoring, 6 trauma ambulances, 1 helicopter for critical evac.
Primary: Gandhi Hospital (25 patients). Secondary: Hitec City Hospital (5 patients).
Secondary dangers: Vibration from nearby rail line causing shifting debris, fuel leak, dust inhalation.
Broadcasted road block warnings, highway diversions, and activated municipal heavy tow cranes.
RESCUE PLAN CONCLUDED. Shoring operations started. Debris removal and search dogs in action.
